name: Buck2 (Meta)
pricing_notes: Open source; free to self-host; no managed service

auth.notes: No auth required; runs locally or in CI

performance.notes: Highly incremental; 10-100x faster than Bazel for incremental builds; Starlark-based

agent_readiness.notes: Meta's next-gen build system successor to Buck. Rewritten in Rust for performance. Starlark build language. Remote execution support. Massive parallelism. Best for large polyglot monorepos. Steeper learning curve than Bazel.

gotchas: Steep learning curve — Starlark + Buck concepts significant investment
Community much smaller than Bazel/Gradle ecosystem
Not suitable for small-medium repos where Nx/Turborepo simpler
best_when: Very large polyglot monorepos (1000+ build targets) where Bazel is too slow
Organizations migrating from Buck (Facebook's previous build system)
Teams needing maximum build parallelism and correctness
summary: Buck2 is Meta's next-generation build system rewritten in Rust, succeeding Buck1. Offers significant performance improvements over Bazel through better incremental builds and remote execution. Uses Starlark build language. Best for very large polyglot monorepos where build speed is critical. Steep learning curve and smaller ecosystem vs Bazel means it's overkill for most teams.
